title: davallia contigua
intro: Davallia contigua, also known as the Split-Fern, is a species of fern native to tropical regions of Asia, particularly in countries such as India, China, and Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Davallia contigua include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating skin conditions such as acne and eczema. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or extracts, which can be consumed as teas or applied topically to the affected area.
